title: Ortus Raster Bundle Manifest
description: Manifest for an ortus raster bundle. Lives at the ZIP root under the fixed name 'ortus-raster.yaml'. Describes one categorical raster source (one or more COG layers) and the value -> attribute mapping that turns a sampled pixel value into Sachdaten.

schema_version.description: Bundle schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

id.description: Stable source id, kebab-case. Becomes the ortus source id; must be unique across all loaded bundles.

description.description: Optional longer description of the dataset.

crs.description: Canonical CRS of every raster in this bundle. The pipeline MUST have reprojected all rasters to this CRS so ortus never reprojects at query time.

layers.description: One or more raster layers (e.g. time slices, scenarios, or tiles already mosaicked into one COG each).

name.description: SPDX id or license name, e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0'.

url.description: Link to the license or source.

layer.$comment: Exactly one of 'mapping' (inline) or 'value_mapping' (sidecar pointer) must be present. The oneOf below enforces this: if both are present, both subschemas match and oneOf fails; if neither is present, neither matches and oneOf fails.

id.description: Layer id, kebab-case, unique within the bundle.

file.description: Relative path to the COG inside the bundle. No leading slash, no parent traversal (validator additionally rejects '..').

nodata.description: Pixel value treated as no-data. A sample equal to nodata yields no feature (no match), not an error.

sampling.description: Resampling at the query point. Categorical rasters MUST use 'nearest' (interpolation would invent classes).

value_mapping.description: Relative path to a sidecar mapping file (.json/.yaml/.yml) for large tables. Same logical shape as the inline 'mapping'. No leading slash, no parent traversal.

mapping.description: Inline value -> attribute table. Keys are integer pixel values written as JSON strings (object keys are always strings). Each value is a flat object of scalar properties that becomes Feature.Properties verbatim.

propertyNames.description: Integer pixel value with no leading zeros, optional leading minus.

attributes.description: Flat set of attribute properties for one pixel value. Scalars only; UTF-8 strings should be quoted in YAML to avoid the 'Norway problem' (no/yes/on becoming booleans).
